contrast-pairs.$description: Valid contrast pairs, keyed by the two CSS color properties in alphabetical order joined with '<>'. Only foregrounds that require contrast against background-color; exempt properties (flood/lighting/stop/box-shadow-color) omitted. Derived from the webref color-property set.
contrast-pairs.$tierResolution: The tier (text vs non-text) is NOT fixed by the property — it comes from the NODE carrying the foreground: does it render text, at what size (large → 4.5:1), text-element or shape? The property only proposes a candidate.
contrast-pairs.$treeResolution: A pair spans the node tree, not one node. The foreground (e.g. color) INHERITS — it may come from the node or an ancestor. The background (background-color) does NOT inherit — the effective background is the nearest ancestor that paints one. So the two colors usually sit on different nodes; parent and child colors both matter when resolving a pair.

-webkit-text-fill-color<>background-colo.$note: The tier (text 7:1/4.5:1 or non-text 3:1) and the exact ratio come from the NODE carrying `-webkit-text-fill-color`: does it render text, and at what size (large text → 4.5:1)? The property only proposes the candidate.
-webkit-text-fill-color<>background-colo.$resolvesAcrossTree: `-webkit-text-fill-color` inherits, so the foreground may come from this node or an ancestor; `background-color` does NOT inherit, so the background is the nearest ancestor that paints one. The two colors usually live on DIFFERENT nodes — parent and child both matter.

-webkit-text-stroke<>background-color.$resolvesAcrossTree: `-webkit-text-stroke` inherits, so the foreground may come from this node or an ancestor; `background-color` does NOT inherit, so the background is the nearest ancestor that paints one.
